translations.idiomatic: Zhou had long since severed himself from the realm. The world did not abandon him only on the day of his death.

sentences.zh: 今臣亦竊悲大王棄千乘之君，必且賜絕命之書，為群臣先，死於東宮也。」

translations.literal: Now your servant also secretly grieves that Your Majesty will throw away the position of a lord of a thousand chariots, and will surely receive a decree of execution, preceding your ministers in death at the eastern palace.'
translations.idiomatic: Now I secretly grieve that Your Majesty will cast away the rank of a lord of a thousand chariots and will surely receive an edict of execution, dying before your ministers in the eastern palace.'

sentences.zh: 於是［王］氣怨結而不揚，涕滿匡而橫流，即起，歷階而去。

translations.literal: At this, the king's breath caught with grief and resentment. Tears filled his eyes and streamed down his face. He rose and descended the steps to leave.
translations.idiomatic: At these words, the king's breath caught in his throat, choked with grief and resentment. Tears filled his eyes and streamed down his face. He rose and walked down the steps without a word.

sentences.zh: 王有孽子不害，最長，王弗愛，王、王后、太子皆不以為子兄數。

translations.literal: The king had an illegitimate son named Buhai, the eldest of his sons, but the king did not love him. The king, the queen, and the Crown Prince all refused to treat him as a son or elder brother.
translations.idiomatic: The king had an illegitimate son named Buhai, the eldest of his sons, whom the king did not care for. Neither the king, the queen, nor the Crown Prince treated him as a son or elder brother.

sentences.zh: 不害有子建，材高有氣，常怨望太子不省其父；

translations.literal: Buhai had a son named Jian, who was talented and spirited. He constantly resented that the Crown Prince paid no attention to his father;
translations.idiomatic: Buhai had a son named Jian, who was able and spirited. Jian harbored a constant resentment that the Crown Prince took no notice of his father;

sentences.zh: 又怨時諸侯皆得分子弟為侯，而淮南獨二子，一為太子，建父獨不得為侯。

translations.literal: he also resented that at the time all other vassal lords were dividing their territories among their sons as marquises, yet Huainan had only two sons, one of whom was the Crown Prince, and Jian's father alone was not granted a marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: he also resented that other vassal lords were allowed to divide their territories among their sons and grant them marquisates, while in Huainan there were only two sons—one already the Crown Prince—and his own father alone received nothing.

sentences.zh: 建陰結交，欲告敗太子，以其父代之。

translations.literal: Jian secretly formed connections, intending to denounce and ruin the Crown Prince so that his father could replace him.
translations.idiomatic: Jian secretly cultivated alliances, planning to denounce and bring down the Crown Prince so that his own father could take his place.

sentences.zh: 太子知之，數捕系而榜笞建。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince learned of this and repeatedly had Jian arrested, imprisoned, and beaten with bamboo rods.
translations.idiomatic: When the Crown Prince learned of this, he had Jian repeatedly arrested and beaten with bamboo rods.

sentences.zh: 建具知太子之謀欲殺漢中尉，即使所善壽春莊芷以元朔六年上書於天子曰：「毒藥苦於口利於病，忠言逆於耳利於行。

translations.literal: Jian was fully aware of the Crown Prince's plot to assassinate the Han Colonel. He therefore sent his friend Zhuang Zhi of Shouchun to submit a memorial to the Son of Heaven in the sixth year of the Yuanshuo era, which said: 'Bitter medicine is hard on the mouth but good for illness; honest counsel is hard on the ear but good for conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Jian was fully aware of the Crown Prince's plot to assassinate the Han Colonel. He therefore had his trusted associate Zhuang Zhi of Shouchun submit a memorial to the emperor in the sixth year of the Yuanshuo era, which read: 'Bitter medicine is unpleasant to the taste but cures disease; honest counsel is harsh to the ear but guides right action.

sentences.zh: 今淮南王孫建，材能高，淮南王王后荼、荼子太子遷常疾害建。

translations.literal: Now Jian, the grandson of the King of Huainan, is a man of great talent. Yet the King of Huainan's Queen Tu and her son Crown Prince Qian constantly persecute and harm Jian.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan's grandson Jian is a man of great ability. Yet Queen Tu and her son Crown Prince Qian have relentlessly persecuted him.

sentences.zh: 建父不害無罪，擅數捕系，欲殺之。

translations.literal: Jian's father Buhai has committed no crime, yet they have repeatedly had him arrested and imprisoned without authority, and wish to kill him.
translations.idiomatic: Jian's father Buhai has committed no crime, yet they have repeatedly had him arrested without cause and wish to have him killed.

sentences.zh: 今建在，可徵問，具知淮南陰事。」

translations.literal: Jian is still alive and can be summoned for questioning. He knows in full the secret affairs of Huainan.'
translations.idiomatic: Jian is still alive and may be summoned for questioning. He knows the full extent of the secret activities in Huainan.'

sentences.zh: 書聞，上以其事下廷尉，廷尉下河南治。

translations.literal: When the memorial was received, the Emperor referred the matter to the Commandant of Justice, who in turn referred it to the Governor of Henan for investigation.
translations.idiomatic: When the memorial reached the emperor, he referred the matter to the Commandant of Justice, who in turn sent it to the Governor of Henan for investigation.

sentences.zh: 是時故辟陽侯孫審卿善丞相公孫弘，怨淮南厲王殺其大父，乃深購淮南事於弘，弘乃疑淮南有畔逆計謀，深窮治其獄。

translations.literal: At that time, Shen Qing, grandson of the former Marquis of Biyang, was on good terms with Chancellor Gongsun Hong. Harboring a grudge against King Li of Huainan for killing his grandfather, he persistently urged Hong to pursue the case against Huainan. Hong therefore suspected that Huainan was harboring plans for rebellion and pushed the investigation to its fullest extent.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Shen Qing, the grandson of the former Marquis of Biyang, was on good terms with Chancellor Gongsun Hong. Shen Qing bore a grudge against King Li of Huainan for having killed his grandfather and persistently urged the chancellor to pursue the Huainan case. Gongsun Hong grew suspicious that Huainan harbored plans for rebellion and pushed the investigation to its fullest extent.

sentences.zh: 河南治建，辭引淮南太子及黨與。

translations.literal: When the Governor of Henan interrogated Jian, his testimony implicated the Crown Prince of Huainan and his associates.
translations.idiomatic: When the Governor of Henan interrogated Jian, his testimony implicated the Crown Prince of Huainan and his circle of conspirators.

sentences.zh: 淮南王患之，欲發，問伍被曰：「漢廷治亂？」

translations.literal: The King of Huainan was alarmed and wished to launch the revolt. He asked Wu Bei: 'Is the Han court in order or in disorder?'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan was alarmed and wanted to launch the revolt at once. He asked Wu Bei, 'Is the Han court well-governed or in disarray?'

sentences.zh: 伍被曰：「天下治。」

translations.literal: Wu Bei said: 'The realm is well-governed.'
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei answered, 'The realm is well-governed.'

sentences.zh: 王意不說，謂伍被曰：「公何以言天下治也？」

translations.literal: The king was displeased and said to Wu Bei: 'On what basis do you say the realm is well-governed?'
translations.idiomatic: The king was displeased and demanded, 'On what grounds do you say the realm is well-governed?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「被竊觀朝廷之政，君臣之義，父子之親，夫婦之別，長幼之序，皆得其理，上之舉錯遵古之道，風俗紀綱未有所缺也。

translations.literal: Bei said: 'Your servant has privately observed the governance of the court. The righteousness between ruler and minister, the affection between father and son, the distinction between husband and wife, the order between elder and younger—all are in their proper place. The actions of the Emperor follow the way of the ancients, and in customs and institutions there is nothing lacking.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied, 'I have privately observed the conduct of the court. The righteousness between ruler and minister, the affection between father and son, the distinction between husband and wife, the order between elder and younger—all are properly maintained. The emperor's measures follow the way of the ancients, and there is no deficiency in customs or institutions.

sentences.zh: 重裝富賈，周流天下，道無不通，故交易之道行。

translations.literal: Merchants with heavy loads and great wealth circulate throughout the realm, and there is no road that is not open. Commerce therefore flourishes.
translations.idiomatic: Wealthy merchants with heavy loads travel freely throughout the realm, and every road is open. Commerce flourishes accordingly.

sentences.zh: 南越賓服，羌僰入獻，東甌入降，廣長榆，開朔方，匈奴折翅傷翼，失援不振。

translations.literal: Nanyue has submitted in allegiance, the Qiang and Bo peoples bring tribute, the Eastern Ou have surrendered, the frontier has been extended to Changyu, Shuofang has been opened, and the Xiongnu have had their wings broken and clipped, losing their allies and unable to recover.
translations.idiomatic: Nanyue has submitted as a vassal, the Qiang and Bo peoples bring tribute, the Eastern Ou have surrendered, the frontier has been extended at Changyu, Shuofang has been opened, and the Xiongnu have had their wings broken and clipped—bereft of allies and unable to recover.

sentences.zh: 雖未及古太平之時，然猶為治也。」

translations.literal: Though it has not yet reached the Great Peace of antiquity, it is still well-governed.'
translations.idiomatic: Though it has not yet reached the Great Peace of antiquity, the realm is nonetheless well-governed.'

sentences.zh: 王怒，被謝死罪。

translations.literal: The king was furious. Bei apologized and begged for death.
translations.idiomatic: The king flew into a rage, and Wu Bei prostrated himself, begging forgiveness for the offense.

sentences.zh: 王又謂被曰：「山東即有兵，漢必使大將軍將而制山東，公以為大將軍何如人也？」

translations.literal: The king then said to Bei: 'If troops were raised east of the mountains, the Han would surely send the Grand General to lead and control the east. What sort of man do you consider the Grand General to be?'
translations.idiomatic: The king then asked Wu Bei, 'If war were to break out east of the mountains, the Han would surely send the Grand General to take command. What kind of man do you take the Grand General to be?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「被所善者黃義，從大將軍擊匈奴，還，告被曰：『大將軍遇士大夫有禮，於士卒有恩，眾皆樂為之用。

translations.literal: Bei said: 'One of your servant's friends, Huang Yi, served under the Grand General in the campaign against the Xiongnu. On his return he told your servant: "The Grand General treats his officers with courtesy and shows kindness to his soldiers. All his men are happy to serve him.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied, 'A friend of mine named Huang Yi served under the Grand General in the campaign against the Xiongnu. When he returned, he told me, "The Grand General treats his officers with courtesy and shows kindness to his soldiers. All his men are glad to serve him.

sentences.zh: 騎上下山若蜚，材干絕人。』

translations.literal: He rides up and down mountains as if flying, and his ability surpasses all others."'
translations.idiomatic: He rides up and down mountains as though he were flying, and his ability surpasses that of any other man."'

sentences.zh: 被以為材能如此，數將習兵，未易當也。

translations.literal: Your servant believes that with such ability, and having led troops and trained in warfare many times, he would not be easy to face.
translations.idiomatic: I believe that with such ability, and with his long experience leading troops and training in warfare, he would not be easy to overcome.

sentences.zh: 及謁者曹梁使長安來，言大將軍號令明，當敵勇敢，常為士卒先。

translations.literal: Furthermore, when the Usher Cao Liang returned from a mission to Chang'an, he said that the Grand General's commands are clear, that he is brave and courageous in the face of the enemy, and that he always leads his soldiers from the front.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, when the Usher Cao Liang returned from a mission to Chang'an, he reported that the Grand General's commands are clear and decisive, that he is fearless in the face of the enemy, and that he always leads his soldiers from the front.

sentences.zh: 休舍，穿井未通，須士卒盡得水，乃敢飲。

translations.literal: When the army rests and a well is being dug but has not yet reached water, he waits until every soldier has had water before he dares drink.
translations.idiomatic: When the army halts to camp and a well is being dug, he waits until every last soldier has had water before he himself takes a drink.

sentences.zh: 軍罷，卒盡已度河，乃度。

translations.literal: When the army disbands, he waits until every soldier has crossed the river before he himself crosses.
translations.idiomatic: When the army breaks camp, he waits until every last soldier has crossed the river before he himself crosses.

sentences.zh: 皇太后所賜金帛，盡以賜軍吏。

translations.literal: The gold and silk bestowed by the Empress Dowager he distributes entirely among the army's officers.
translations.idiomatic: All the gold and silk bestowed upon him by the Empress Dowager he distributes entirely among his officers.

sentences.zh: 雖古名將弗過也。」

translations.literal: Not even the famous generals of antiquity surpassed him.'
translations.idiomatic: Even the renowned generals of antiquity could not surpass him.'

sentences.zh: 王默然。

translations.literal: The king fell silent.
translations.idiomatic: The king was silenced.

sentences.zh: 淮南王見建已徵治，恐國陰事且覺，欲發，被又以為難，乃復問被曰：「公以為吳興兵是邪非也？」

translations.literal: The King of Huainan saw that Jian had been summoned for investigation and feared that the kingdom's secret plots would be uncovered. He wished to launch the revolt, but Bei again considered it difficult. The king then asked Bei: 'Do you think Wu was right or wrong to raise troops?'
translations.idiomatic: Seeing that Jian had been summoned for investigation, the King of Huainan feared the kingdom's secret plots were about to be exposed. He wanted to launch the revolt at once, but Wu Bei again urged caution. The king asked him, 'Do you believe Wu was right or wrong to raise his armies?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「以為非也。

translations.literal: Bei said: 'I consider it wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied, 'I believe he was wrong.

sentences.zh: 吳王至富貴也，舉事不當，身死丹徒，頭足異處，子孫無遺類。

translations.literal: The King of Wu was supremely wealthy and noble, yet his undertaking was improper. He died at Dantu, his head and feet in different places, and his descendants were wiped out without a trace.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Wu was a man of supreme wealth and rank, yet his enterprise was ill-conceived. He perished at Dantu with his head severed from his body, and every last one of his descendants was exterminated.

sentences.zh: 臣聞吳王悔之甚。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the King of Wu deeply regretted it.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that the King of Wu came to regret it bitterly.

sentences.zh: 願王孰慮之，無為吳王之所悔。」

translations.literal: I hope Your Majesty will consider carefully and not bring upon himself the same regret as the King of Wu.'
translations.idiomatic: I beg Your Majesty to think this through carefully and not bring upon yourself the same regret as the King of Wu.'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「男子之所死者一言耳。

translations.literal: The king said: 'What a man dies for is but a single word.
translations.idiomatic: The king said, 'A man stakes his life on a single resolve.

sentences.zh: 且吳何知反，漢將一日過成皋者四十餘人。

translations.literal: Besides, what did Wu know of rebellion? More than forty Han generals passed through Chenggao in a single day.
translations.idiomatic: Besides, what did Wu know about waging a rebellion? More than forty Han generals passed through the Chenggao pass in a single day.

sentences.zh: 今我令樓緩先要成皋之口，周被下潁川兵塞轘轅、伊闕之道，陳定發南陽兵守武關。

translations.literal: Now I will order Lou Huan to seize the pass at Chenggao first, Zhou Bei to lead troops from Yingchuan to block the roads at Huanyuan and Yique, and Chen Ding to mobilize the troops of Nanyang to hold Wuguan.
translations.idiomatic: I will order Lou Huan to seize the Chenggao pass at the outset, Zhou Bei to lead the troops of Yingchuan to block the roads at Huanyuan and Yique, and Chen Ding to mobilize the forces of Nanyang to hold Wuguan.

sentences.zh: 河南太守獨有雒陽耳，何足憂。

translations.literal: The Governor of Henan has only Luoyang. What is there to worry about?
translations.idiomatic: The Governor of Henan holds only Luoyang. What is there to worry about?

sentences.zh: 然此北尚有臨晉關、河東、上黨與河內、趙國。

translations.literal: To the north, however, there are still the Linjin Pass, Hedong, Shangdang, Henei, and the Kingdom of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: To the north, however, there remain the Linjin Pass, Hedong, Shangdang, Henei, and the Kingdom of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 人言曰『絕成皋之口，天下不通』。

translations.literal: As the saying goes, 'Cut off the pass at Chenggao, and the realm is severed.'
translations.idiomatic: As the saying goes, 'Cut off the pass at Chenggao, and the empire is cut in two.'

sentences.zh: 據三川之險，招山東之兵，舉事如此，公以為何如？」

translations.literal: Holding the strategic position of the Three Rivers, we can summon the forces east of the mountains. With a campaign launched in this manner, what do you think?'
translations.idiomatic: Holding the strategic ground of the Three Rivers, we can rally the armies from east of the mountains. With such a plan, what do you think?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「臣見其禍，未見其福也。」

translations.literal: Bei said: 'Your servant sees the disaster in it but not the good fortune.'
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied, 'I see only disaster in this, not good fortune.'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「左吳、趙賢、朱驕如皆以為有福，什事九成，公獨以為有禍無福，何也？」

translations.literal: The king said: 'Zuo Wu, Zhao Xian, and Zhu Jiaoru all believe there will be good fortune, that nine out of ten things will succeed. Why do you alone see disaster and no good fortune?'
translations.idiomatic: The king said, 'Zuo Wu, Zhao Xian, and Zhu Jiaoru all believe our fortunes are favorable and that nine chances out of ten will succeed. Why are you alone in seeing only disaster?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「大王之群臣近幸素能使眾者，皆前系詔獄，餘無可用者。」

translations.literal: Bei said: 'Those among Your Majesty's ministers and favorites who were capable of commanding men have all been previously imprisoned by imperial warrant. None of the remainder are fit for the task.'
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied, 'Those among Your Majesty's ministers and trusted men who were capable of commanding a following have all been arrested by imperial warrant. None of those who remain are fit for such an undertaking.'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「陳勝、吳廣無立錐之地，千人之聚，起於大澤，奮臂大呼而天下響應，西至於戲而兵百二十萬。

translations.literal: The king said: 'Chen Sheng and Wu Guang had not so much as a patch of land to stand an awl on, no gathering of more than a thousand men. They rose at Daze, raised their arms and gave a great shout, and the entire realm responded. By the time they reached Xi in the west, their army numbered one million two hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The king said, 'Chen Sheng and Wu Guang did not possess so much as a patch of ground on which to stand an awl, nor a gathering of more than a thousand men. They rose at Daze, raised their arms with a great shout, and the entire realm rallied to their call. By the time they reached Xi in the west, their armies numbered one million two hundred thousand.

sentences.zh: 今吾國雖小，然而勝兵者可得十餘萬，非直適戍之眾，釠鑿棘矜也，公何以言有禍無福？」

translations.literal: Now although my kingdom is small, I can muster more than a hundred thousand battle-ready troops—not a mere rabble of conscript border guards armed with pikes and halberds. On what basis do you say there is disaster but no good fortune?'
translations.idiomatic: Now, although my kingdom is small, I can muster more than a hundred thousand battle-ready troops—not a rabble of frontier conscripts wielding makeshift weapons. How can you say there is only disaster and no good fortune?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「往者秦為無道，殘賊天下。

translations.literal: Bei said: 'In the past, the Qin acted without the Way and brutalized the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied, 'In the past, the Qin was a lawless tyrant that brutalized the realm.

sentences.zh: 興萬乘之駕，作阿房之宮，收太半之賦，發閭左之戍，父不寧子，兄不便弟，政苛刑峻，天下熬然若焦，民皆引領而望，傾耳而聽，悲號仰天，叩心而怨上，故陳勝大呼，天下響應。

translations.literal: It raised the carriages of ten thousand chariots, built the Epang Palace, collected more than half of the harvest in taxes, and conscripted the poor for garrison duty. Fathers could not provide for their sons, and elder brothers could not help their younger brothers. Government was harsh and punishments severe. The realm seethed as though scorched by fire. The people craned their necks and looked on, pricked up their ears and listened, wailed to Heaven, and beat their chests in anger at their rulers. Therefore when Chen Sheng raised his great shout, the entire realm responded.
translations.idiomatic: It mounted the carriages of ten thousand chariots, built the Epang Palace, collected more than half the harvest in taxes, and conscripted the poor for garrison duty. Fathers could not provide for their sons, and elder brothers could not support their younger brothers. Government was harsh and punishments draconian. The realm seethed as though scorched by fire. The people craned their necks in longing, pricked up their ears to listen, wailed to Heaven, and beat their chests in rage against their rulers. And so when Chen Sheng raised his great shout, the entire realm answered.

sentences.zh: 當今陛下臨制天下，一齊海內，汎愛蒸庶，布德施惠。

translations.literal: Now His Majesty rules over the realm, unifying all within the seas, showing universal love to the common people, and spreading virtue and bestowing favors.
translations.idiomatic: Today, the emperor governs the realm, has unified all within the seas, shows universal love to the common people, and spreads virtue and bestows favors.

sentences.zh: 口雖未言，聲疾雷霆，令雖未出，化馳如神，心有所懷，威動萬里，下之應上，猶影響也。

translations.literal: Though his lips have not yet spoken, his voice is swifter than thunder. Though his edicts have not yet been issued, his transforming influence speeds like a spirit. When his heart holds a purpose, his authority moves ten thousand li. The response of those below to the one above is like a shadow or an echo.
translations.idiomatic: Before the emperor's lips even part, his voice resounds swifter than thunder. Before his edicts are even issued, his transforming influence races forth like a spirit. When his heart conceives a purpose, his authority moves ten thousand li. The people respond to their sovereign as surely as a shadow follows a form or an echo follows a sound.

sentences.zh: 而大將軍材能不特章邯、楊熊也。

translations.literal: And the Grand General's ability is not merely that of a Zhang Han or a Yang Xiong.
translations.idiomatic: And the Grand General's ability is not merely that of a Zhang Han or a Yang Xiong.

sentences.zh: 大王以陳勝、吳廣諭之，被以為過矣。」

translations.literal: For Your Majesty to draw an analogy with Chen Sheng and Wu Guang, your servant considers this a grave error.'
translations.idiomatic: For Your Majesty to compare your position with that of Chen Sheng and Wu Guang is, I believe, a grave error.'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「茍如公言，不可徼幸邪？」

translations.literal: The king said: 'If things are as you say, is there truly no chance of success?'
translations.idiomatic: The king asked, 'If what you say is true, is there truly no hope of success?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「被有愚計。」

translations.literal: Bei said: 'Your servant has a foolish plan.'
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei said, 'I do have a plan, though it is a desperate one.'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「柰何？」

translations.literal: The king said: 'What is it?'
translations.idiomatic: The king asked, 'What is it?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「當今諸侯無異心，百姓無怨氣。

translations.literal: Bei said: 'At present the vassal lords harbor no disloyal thoughts, and the people have no cause for resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei said, 'At present, the vassal lords harbor no disloyal thoughts, and the people have no grievances.

sentences.zh: 朔方之郡田地廣，水草美，民徙者不足以實其地。

translations.literal: The commandery of Shuofang has vast farmland with fine water and grass, but the settlers sent there are not enough to fill the land.
translations.idiomatic: The commandery of Shuofang has vast farmland with excellent water and pasture, but the settlers sent there are too few to fill it.

sentences.zh: 臣之愚計，可偽為丞相御史請書，徙郡國豪桀任俠及有耐罪以上，赦令除其罪，產五十萬以上者，皆徙其家屬朔方之郡，益發甲卒，急其會日。

translations.literal: My humble plan is this: forge a petition in the names of the Chancellor and the Grandee Secretary, ordering the resettlement to the Shuofang commandery of powerful local lords, chivalrous men, and those convicted of crimes carrying sentences of shaving or above—pardoning and clearing their crimes—as well as all families with property of five hundred thousand cash or more. At the same time, increase the levy of armored soldiers and set an urgent date for their assembly.
translations.idiomatic: My plan is this: forge a petition in the names of the Chancellor and the Grandee Secretary, ordering that powerful local magnates, men of arms, and all those convicted of crimes carrying sentences of branding or above be relocated to the commandery of Shuofang, their crimes pardoned and cleared—together with all families possessing property worth five hundred thousand cash or more. Simultaneously, increase the levy of armored soldiers and set an urgent date for their assembly.

sentences.zh: 又偽為左右都司空上林中都官詔獄（逮）書，［逮］諸侯太子幸臣。

translations.literal: Also forge warrants from the Left and Right Directors of Public Works, the Shanglin Park administration, and the central offices, ordering the arrest and imprisonment of the Crown Princes and favored ministers of the vassal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Then forge warrants from the Left and Right Directors of Public Works, the Shanglin Park administration, and the central judicial offices, ordering the arrest of the Crown Princes and favored ministers of the vassal lords.

sentences.zh: 如此則民怨，諸侯懼，即使辯武隨而說之，儻可徼幸什得一乎？」

translations.literal: In this way the people will be outraged and the vassal lords terrified. Then send eloquent and martial men to follow up and persuade them. Perhaps by a stroke of luck one chance in ten might succeed?'
translations.idiomatic: If this is done, the people will be outraged and the vassal lords terrified. Then send eloquent and capable men to rally them to our cause. Perhaps by a stroke of luck we might achieve one chance in ten of success?'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「此可也。

translations.literal: The king said: 'This is feasible.
translations.idiomatic: The king said, 'This could work.

sentences.zh: 雖然，吾以為不至若此。」

translations.literal: Even so, I do not think it will come to that.'
translations.idiomatic: Still, I do not believe it will come to that.'

sentences.zh: 於是王乃令官奴入宮，作皇帝璽，丞相、御史、大將軍、軍吏、中二千石、都官令、丞印，及旁近郡太守、都尉印，漢使節法冠，欲如伍被計。

translations.literal: Thereupon the king ordered palace slaves to enter the workshop and forge the imperial seal, along with the seals of the Chancellor, the Grandee Secretary, the Grand General, military officers, officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, metropolitan office heads and their assistants, as well as the seals of the governors and commandants of neighboring commanderies, and the tallies and ceremonial caps of Han envoys, intending to follow Wu Bei's plan.
translations.idiomatic: The king then ordered palace slaves into the workshops to forge the imperial seal, along with the seals of the Chancellor, the Grandee Secretary, the Grand General, military officers, officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, heads of metropolitan offices and their assistants, governors and commandants of neighboring commanderies, and the tallies and caps used by Han envoys—all in accordance with Wu Bei's plan.

sentences.zh: 使人偽得罪而西，事大將軍、丞相；

translations.literal: He sent agents who pretended to have committed crimes to travel west and insinuate themselves into the service of the Grand General and the Chancellor;
translations.idiomatic: He sent agents who posed as criminals to travel west and infiltrate the households of the Grand General and the Chancellor;

sentences.zh: 一日發兵，使人即刺殺大將軍青，而說丞相下之，如發蒙耳。

translations.literal: when the day came to raise troops, they would assassinate Grand General Qing at once and persuade the Chancellor to surrender, as easy as lifting a cover.
translations.idiomatic: on the day the revolt was launched, these agents would assassinate Grand General Qing and persuade the Chancellor to capitulate—as simple as lifting a veil.

sentences.zh: 王欲發國中兵，恐其相、二千石不聽。

translations.literal: The king wished to mobilize the troops within his kingdom but feared his chancellor and officials of two-thousand-bushel rank would not comply.
translations.idiomatic: The king wanted to mobilize the forces within his kingdom, but feared that his chancellor and the officials of two-thousand-bushel rank would refuse to comply.

sentences.zh: 王乃與伍被謀，先殺相、二千石；

translations.literal: The king then plotted with Wu Bei to first kill the chancellor and the officials of two-thousand-bushel rank;
translations.idiomatic: The king then plotted with Wu Bei to first assassinate the chancellor and the officials of two-thousand-bushel rank;

sentences.zh: 偽失火宮中，相、二千石救火，至即殺之。

translations.literal: they would stage a false fire in the palace, and when the chancellor and officials came to fight the fire, they would kill them on the spot.
translations.idiomatic: they would set a false fire in the palace, and when the chancellor and the officials rushed to fight the blaze, they would be killed on the spot.

sentences.zh: 計未決，又欲令人衣求盜衣，持羽檄，從東方來，呼曰「南越兵入界」，欲因以發兵。

translations.literal: Before the plan was settled, they also considered having men dress in the uniforms of bandit hunters, carrying feathered dispatches, come from the east shouting 'Nanyue troops have invaded the border!' in order to use this as a pretext to raise troops.
translations.idiomatic: Before the plan was finalized, they also devised another scheme: to have men disguised as bandit hunters carrying feathered dispatches ride in from the east, shouting, 'Nanyue troops have crossed the border!' They would then use this alarm as a pretext to mobilize their forces.

sentences.zh: 乃使人至廬江、會稽為求盜，未發。

translations.literal: They sent men to Lujiang and Kuaiji to serve as bandit hunters, but had not yet launched the plan.
translations.idiomatic: They sent agents to Lujiang and Kuaiji to pose as bandit hunters, but the plan had not yet been set in motion.

sentences.zh: 王問伍被曰：「吾舉兵西鄉，諸侯必有應我者；

translations.literal: The king asked Wu Bei: 'If I raise troops and march west, some of the vassal lords will surely respond to me;
translations.idiomatic: The king asked Wu Bei, 'If I raise my armies and march west, some of the vassal lords will surely rally to my cause;

sentences.zh: 即無應，柰何？」

translations.literal: but if none respond, what then?'
translations.idiomatic: but what if none do?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「南收衡山以擊廬江，有尋陽之船，守下雉之城，結九江之浦，絕豫章之口，彊弩臨江而守，以禁南郡之下，東收江都、會稽，南通勁越，屈彊江淮閒，猶可得延歲月之壽。」

translations.literal: Bei said: 'To the south, take Hengshan and use it to attack Lujiang. Seize the boats at Xunyang, hold the fortress of Xiazhi, secure the ports of the Nine Rivers, cut off the mouth of Yuzhang, line the banks of the Yangtze with powerful crossbowmen to hold back the troops of Nan Commandery to the south. To the east, take Jiangdu and Kuaiji. To the south, establish contact with the powerful Yue. Entrenching our forces between the Yangtze and the Huai, we could still prolong our survival for some years.'
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied, 'Take Hengshan to the south and use it as a base to attack Lujiang. Seize the boats at Xunyang, hold the fortress of Xiazhi, secure the ports of the Nine Rivers, cut off the mouth of Yuzhang, and station powerful crossbowmen along the Yangtze to block any advance from Nan Commandery. To the east, take Jiangdu and Kuaiji. To the south, make contact with the powerful Yue kingdoms. Entrenched between the Yangtze and the Huai, we could prolong our survival for some years.'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「善，無以易此。

translations.literal: The king said: 'Excellent. There is nothing better than this plan.
translations.idiomatic: The king said, 'Excellent. There is no better plan than this.

sentences.zh: 急則走越耳。」

translations.literal: If all else fails, we can always flee to Yue.'
translations.idiomatic: And in the worst case, we can always flee to Yue.'

sentences.zh: 於是廷尉以王孫建辭連淮南王太子遷聞。

translations.literal: At this time, the Commandant of Justice reported that the testimony of the king's grandson Jian implicated the Crown Prince Qian of Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile, the Commandant of Justice reported that the testimony of the king's grandson Jian had implicated the Crown Prince of Huainan, Qian.

sentences.zh: 上遣廷尉監因拜淮南中尉，逮捕太子。

translations.literal: The Emperor dispatched the Deputy Commandant of Justice, simultaneously appointing him Colonel of Huainan, to arrest the Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor dispatched the Deputy Commandant of Justice, simultaneously appointing him Colonel of Huainan, to arrest the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 至淮南，淮南王聞，與太子謀召相、二千石，欲殺而發兵。

translations.literal: When the envoy arrived in Huainan, the King of Huainan heard of it and plotted with the Crown Prince to summon the chancellor and the officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, intending to kill them and raise troops.
translations.idiomatic: When word reached the King of Huainan that the envoy had arrived, he plotted with the Crown Prince to summon the chancellor and the officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, planning to kill them and launch the revolt.

sentences.zh: 召相，相至；

translations.literal: They summoned the chancellor, and the chancellor came;
translations.idiomatic: They summoned the chancellor, and he came;

sentences.zh: 內史以出為解。

translations.literal: the Director of the Capital excused himself, saying he had gone out.
translations.idiomatic: but the Director of the Capital excused himself on the grounds that he was away.

sentences.zh: 中尉曰：「臣受詔使，不得見王。」

translations.literal: The Colonel said: 'Your servant has received an imperial edict and is on a mission. I am unable to see the king.'
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel replied, 'I have received an imperial edict and am on official business. I am unable to see the king.'

sentences.zh: 王念獨殺相而內史中尉不來，無益也，即罷相。

translations.literal: The king reflected that killing the chancellor alone while the Director of the Capital and the Colonel refused to come would serve no purpose, and so he dismissed the chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The king realized that killing the chancellor alone while the Director of the Capital and the Colonel refused to appear would serve no purpose, and so he let the chancellor go.

sentences.zh: 王猶豫，計未決。

translations.literal: The king hesitated, and the plan remained unsettled.
translations.idiomatic: The king wavered, unable to reach a decision.

sentences.zh: 太子念所坐者謀刺漢中尉，所與謀者已死，以為口絕，乃謂王曰：「群臣可用者皆前系，今無足與舉事者。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince reflected that his offense was plotting to assassinate the Han Colonel, and that those who had been part of the plot were already dead, so there was no one left to testify. He then said to the king: 'All the ministers who could be of use have already been arrested. Now there is no one fit to launch the enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince considered that his offense was the plot to assassinate the Han Colonel, and since all the co-conspirators were dead, there was no one left to testify against him. He said to the king, 'All the ministers who could have been of use have already been arrested. There is no one left capable of carrying out the enterprise.

sentences.zh: 王以非時發，恐無功，臣願會逮。」

translations.literal: If Your Majesty launches the revolt at the wrong time, I fear it will fail. Your servant is willing to submit to arrest.'
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty launches the revolt now, at the wrong time, I fear it will fail. I am willing to submit to arrest.'

sentences.zh: 王亦偷欲休，即許太子。

translations.literal: The king also secretly wished to abandon the plan and agreed with the Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: The king, too, was secretly eager to give up the plan and consented to the Crown Prince's proposal.

sentences.zh: 太子即自剄，不殊。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince then tried to cut his own throat but failed to sever it completely.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince then attempted to cut his own throat but did not manage to kill himself.

sentences.zh: 伍被自詣吏，因告與淮南王謀反，反蹤跡具如此。

translations.literal: Wu Bei went of his own accord to the authorities and reported the conspiracy with the King of Huainan to rebel. He laid out the full trail of the rebellion as described.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei surrendered himself to the authorities and confessed to the conspiracy with the King of Huainan, laying bare the full extent of the plot as described.

sentences.zh: 吏因捕太子、王后，圍王宮，盡求捕王所與謀反賓客在國中者，索得反具以聞。

translations.literal: The authorities thereupon arrested the Crown Prince and the queen, surrounded the royal palace, and sought to capture all the retainers who had conspired with the king and were still within the kingdom. They seized the instruments of rebellion and reported everything to the throne.
translations.idiomatic: The authorities then arrested the Crown Prince and the queen, surrounded the royal palace, and hunted down all the conspirators still within the kingdom. They seized the implements of rebellion and reported everything to the throne.

sentences.zh: 上下公卿治，所連引與淮南王謀反列侯二千石豪傑數千人，皆以罪輕重受誅。

translations.literal: The Emperor referred the case to the ministers for trial. Thousands of people were implicated through their connection with the King of Huainan's rebellion—marquises, officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, and men of prominence—all of whom were punished according to the severity of their crimes.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor referred the case to the ministers for adjudication. Thousands of people were implicated in the King of Huainan's conspiracy—marquises, officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, and men of prominence—all punished according to the gravity of their offenses.

sentences.zh: 衡山王賜，淮南王弟也，當坐收，有司請逮捕衡山王。

translations.literal: The King of Hengshan, Ci, was the brother of the King of Huainan and should have been implicated. The authorities requested permission to arrest the King of Hengshan.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Hengshan, Ci, was the brother of the King of Huainan and should by law have been implicated. The authorities petitioned for permission to arrest him.

sentences.zh: 天子曰：「諸侯各以其國為本，不當相坐。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven said: 'Each vassal lord has his own kingdom as his foundation. They should not be held jointly guilty.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, 'Each vassal lord has his own kingdom. They should not be held jointly accountable.

sentences.zh: 與諸侯王列侯會肄丞相諸侯議。」

translations.literal: Let the vassal kings and marquises assemble to deliberate with the Chancellor on the matter.'
translations.idiomatic: Let the vassal kings and marquises assemble with the Chancellor to deliberate on the matter.'

sentences.zh: 趙王彭祖、列侯臣讓等四十三人議，皆曰：「淮南王安甚大逆無道，謀反明白，當伏誅。」

translations.literal: King Pengzu of Zhao, the Marquis Rang, and forty-three others deliberated and all said: 'The King of Huainan, An, is guilty of extreme treason and lawlessness. His conspiracy is clearly proven and he deserves to be put to death.'
translations.idiomatic: King Pengzu of Zhao, the Marquis Rang, and forty-three others deliberated and unanimously declared, 'The King of Huainan, An, is guilty of the most extreme treason and lawlessness. His conspiracy is clearly established, and he deserves to be put to death.'

sentences.zh: 膠西王臣端議曰：「淮南王安廢法行邪，懷詐偽心，以亂天下，熒惑百姓，倍畔宗廟，妄作妖言。

translations.literal: King Duan of Jiaoxi submitted the following opinion: 'The King of Huainan, An, has cast aside the law and practiced wickedness, harbored deceitful and treacherous intentions, sought to throw the realm into disorder, deluded the common people, betrayed the ancestral temples, and fabricated wild and seditious talk.
translations.idiomatic: King Duan of Jiaoxi submitted the following opinion: 'The King of Huainan, An, has cast aside the law and practiced wickedness, harbored deceit in his heart, sought to plunge the empire into chaos, deluded the common people, betrayed the ancestral temples, and spread wild and seditious talk.

sentences.zh: 春秋曰『臣無將，將而誅』。

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn Annals say, "A minister must not harbor intentions; one who harbors them shall be put to death."
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn Annals declare, "A minister must not plot against his lord; he who plots shall be put to death."

sentences.zh: 安罪重於將，謀反形已定。

translations.literal: An's crime is greater than mere plotting; the form of his rebellion has been established.
translations.idiomatic: An's crime goes beyond mere intent; the evidence of his rebellion is conclusive.

sentences.zh: 臣端所見其書節印圖及他逆無道事驗明白，甚大逆無道，當伏其法。

translations.literal: Your servant Duan has seen the documents, tallies, seals, maps, and other evidence of his treasonous and lawless activities. They are manifestly clear. His crimes constitute the gravest treason and he should suffer the full penalty of the law.
translations.idiomatic: I have personally examined the documents, tallies, seals, maps, and other evidence of his treasonous activities, and the proof is unmistakable. His crimes constitute the gravest treason, and he must suffer the full penalty of the law.

sentences.zh: 而論國吏二百石以上及比者，宗室近幸臣不在法中者，不能相教，當皆免官削爵為士伍，毋得宦為吏。

translations.literal: As for the officials of the kingdom ranked at two hundred bushels or above and those of equivalent rank, as well as members of the imperial clan and favored ministers who were not directly implicated—since they failed to instruct and correct him—they should all be dismissed from office, stripped of their noble ranks, and reduced to commoners, and forbidden to hold office as officials.
translations.idiomatic: As for the kingdom's officials ranked at two hundred bushels or above and those of equivalent rank, as well as members of the imperial clan and favored ministers who were not directly implicated in the plot—since they failed in their duty to counsel and correct him—they should all be dismissed from office, stripped of their ranks, reduced to commoner status, and forbidden from ever holding office again.

sentences.zh: 其非吏，他贖死金二斤八兩。

translations.literal: Those who are not officials shall redeem the death penalty with two catties and eight ounces of gold.
translations.idiomatic: Those who are not officials shall pay a fine of two catties and eight ounces of gold in commutation of the death penalty.

sentences.zh: 以章臣安之罪，使天下明知臣子之道，毋敢復有邪僻倍畔之意。」

translations.literal: Thus shall the crimes of the minister An be made manifest, so that all the realm may clearly understand the duties of a minister and son, and none shall ever again dare harbor wicked or treasonous intentions.'
translations.idiomatic: In this way the crimes of An shall be made manifest, so that all the empire may understand the duties of a minister and a son, and none shall ever again dare entertain wicked or treasonous designs.'

sentences.zh: 丞相弘、廷尉湯等以聞，天子使宗正以符節治王。

translations.literal: Chancellor Hong, Commandant of Justice Tang, and the others reported this to the throne. The Son of Heaven dispatched the Director of the Imperial Clan, bearing official tallies, to try the king.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Gongsun Hong, Commandant of Justice Zhang Tang, and the others presented these findings to the throne. The emperor dispatched the Director of the Imperial Clan with official tallies to pronounce judgment on the king.

sentences.zh: 未至，淮南王安自剄殺。

translations.literal: Before the envoy arrived, the King of Huainan, An, cut his own throat and died.
translations.idiomatic: Before the envoy could arrive, the King of Huainan, An, cut his own throat and died.

sentences.zh: 王后荼、太子遷諸所與謀反者皆族。

translations.literal: Queen Tu, Crown Prince Qian, and all those who had conspired in the rebellion were executed along with their clans.
translations.idiomatic: Queen Tu, Crown Prince Qian, and all who had taken part in the conspiracy were executed along with their entire clans.

sentences.zh: 天子以伍被雅辭多引漢之美，欲勿誅。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven, noting that Wu Bei's eloquent words had often praised the virtues of the Han, wished to spare him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, noting that Wu Bei had so often spoken eloquently in praise of the Han's virtues, wished to spare his life.

sentences.zh: 廷尉湯曰：「被首為王畫反謀，被罪無赦。」

translations.literal: Commandant of Justice Tang said: 'Bei was the first to devise the plan of rebellion for the king. His crime is unpardonable.'
translations.idiomatic: Commandant of Justice Zhang Tang objected, 'Wu Bei was the first to devise the plan of rebellion for the king. His crime admits of no pardon.'

sentences.zh: 遂誅被。

translations.literal: Wu Bei was therefore executed.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei was put to death.

sentences.zh: 國除為九江郡。

translations.literal: The kingdom was abolished and made into the Commandery of Jiujiang.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Huainan was abolished and converted into the Commandery of Jiujiang.

sentences.zh: 衡山王賜，王后乘舒生子三人，長男爽為太子，次男孝，次女無采。

translations.literal: The King of Hengshan, Ci, had a queen named Cheng Shu who bore him three children: the eldest son Shuang, who was the Crown Prince; the second son Xiao; and a daughter Wucai.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Hengshan, Ci, had a queen named Cheng Shu who bore him three children: the eldest son, Shuang, who was made Crown Prince; a second son, Xiao; and a daughter, Wucai.

sentences.zh: 又姬徐來生子男女四人，美人厥姬生子二人。

translations.literal: He also had a concubine named Xu Lai who bore four sons and daughters, and a beauty named Jue Ji who bore two children.
translations.idiomatic: A concubine named Xu Lai bore him four children, and another consort named Jue Ji bore him two.

sentences.zh: 衡山王、淮南王兄弟相責望禮節，閒不相能。

translations.literal: The King of Hengshan and the King of Huainan, though brothers, reproached each other over matters of courtesy and ritual, and there was discord between them.
translations.idiomatic: Though they were brothers, the King of Hengshan and the King of Huainan reproached each other over matters of protocol, and there was constant friction between them.

sentences.zh: 衡山王聞淮南王作為畔逆反具，亦心結賓客以應之，恐為所并。

translations.literal: When the King of Hengshan heard that the King of Huainan was preparing the instruments of rebellion, he too secretly cultivated retainers to support him, fearing he would be swallowed up.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Hengshan learned that the King of Huainan was preparing for rebellion, he too began secretly cultivating retainers of his own in response, fearing that he would be swallowed up by his brother.

sentences.zh: 元光六年，衡山王入朝，其謁者衛慶有方術，欲上書事天子，王怒，故劾慶死罪，彊榜服之。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of the Yuanguang era, the King of Hengshan went to court. His usher Wei Qing possessed knowledge of magical arts and wished to submit a memorial offering his services to the Son of Heaven. The king was furious and had Qing charged with a capital crime, forcing a confession through flogging.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of the Yuanguang era, the King of Hengshan traveled to court. His usher, Wei Qing, who was skilled in the magical arts, wished to submit a memorial offering his services to the emperor. The king was infuriated and had Wei Qing charged with a capital offense, extorting a confession through flogging.

sentences.zh: 衡山內史以為非是，卻其獄。

translations.literal: The Director of the Capital of Hengshan considered this unjust and rejected the case.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of the Capital of Hengshan, judging this unjust, overturned the conviction.

sentences.zh: 王使人上書告內史，內史治，言王不直。

translations.literal: The king sent someone to submit a memorial accusing the Director of the Capital. When the Director of the Capital was investigated, he declared the king to be in the wrong.
translations.idiomatic: The king sent someone to submit a memorial accusing the Director of the Capital, but when the director was investigated, he declared the king to be in the wrong.

sentences.zh: 王又數侵奪人田，壞人冢以為田。

translations.literal: The king also repeatedly seized people's fields and destroyed their tombs to make farmland.
translations.idiomatic: The king also repeatedly seized the fields of the common people and destroyed their tombs to convert the land into farmland.

sentences.zh: 有司請逮治衡山王。

translations.literal: The authorities requested permission to arrest and try the King of Hengshan.
translations.idiomatic: The authorities petitioned for the arrest and trial of the King of Hengshan.

sentences.zh: 天子不許，為置吏二百石以上。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven did not approve but instead appointed officials ranked at two hundred bushels or above to serve in the kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor refused but instead assigned officials of two-hundred-bushel rank or above to keep watch over the kingdom.

sentences.zh: 衡山王以此恚，與奚慈、張廣昌謀，求能為兵法候星氣者，日夜從容王密謀反事。

translations.literal: The King of Hengshan was enraged by this. He conspired with Xi Ci and Zhang Guangchang, seeking men skilled in military strategy and the observation of celestial signs. Day and night they deliberated in secret on the plans for rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Hengshan was enraged. He conspired with Xi Ci and Zhang Guangchang, seeking men versed in military strategy and the reading of celestial omens. Day and night they deliberated in secret on their plans for rebellion.

sentences.zh: 王后乘舒死，立徐來為王后。

translations.literal: When Queen Cheng Shu died, Xu Lai was made queen.
translations.idiomatic: When Queen Cheng Shu died, the concubine Xu Lai was elevated to queen.

sentences.zh: 厥姬俱幸。

translations.literal: Jue Ji was also favored.
translations.idiomatic: The consort Jue Ji was equally favored by the king.

sentences.zh: 兩人相妒，厥姬乃惡王后徐來於太子曰：「徐來使婢蠱道殺太子母。」

translations.literal: The two women were jealous of each other. Jue Ji slandered Queen Xu Lai to the Crown Prince, saying: 'Xu Lai had a slave girl use sorcery to kill the Crown Prince's mother.'
translations.idiomatic: The two women were consumed with jealousy of each other. Jue Ji poisoned the Crown Prince's mind against Queen Xu Lai, telling him, 'Xu Lai had a maidservant use black magic to murder your mother.'

sentences.zh: 太子心怨徐來。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince harbored a grudge against Xu Lai.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince nursed a deep grudge against Xu Lai.

sentences.zh: 徐來兄至衡山，太子與飲，以刃刺傷王后兄。

translations.literal: When Xu Lai's brother came to Hengshan, the Crown Prince drank with him and then stabbed and wounded the queen's brother with a blade.
translations.idiomatic: When Xu Lai's brother visited Hengshan, the Crown Prince drank with him and then stabbed and wounded him.

sentences.zh: 王后怨怒，數毀惡太子於王。

translations.literal: The queen was furious and resentful, and repeatedly slandered the Crown Prince to the king.
translations.idiomatic: The queen was furious and repeatedly slandered the Crown Prince before the king.

sentences.zh: 太子女弟無采，嫁棄歸，與奴姦，又與客姦。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince's younger sister Wucai had been married and then divorced and returned home. She had an affair with a slave and then with a guest.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince's younger sister, Wucai, had been married and divorced, and upon returning home had affairs with a slave and then with a guest.

sentences.zh: 太子數讓無采，無采怒，不與太子通。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince repeatedly rebuked Wucai. Wucai was angry and ceased contact with the Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince repeatedly reproached Wucai for her conduct. Wucai was furious and cut off all contact with her brother.

sentences.zh: 王后聞之，即善遇無采。

translations.literal: When the queen heard of this, she began treating Wucai with great kindness.
translations.idiomatic: When the queen learned of this rift, she began to treat Wucai with conspicuous kindness.

sentences.zh: 無采及中兄孝少失母，附王后，王后以計愛之，與共毀太子，王以故數擊笞太子。

translations.literal: Wucai and her middle brother Xiao had lost their mother at a young age and attached themselves to the queen. The queen calculated and showed them affection, and together they slandered the Crown Prince. The king on this account repeatedly beat and flogged the Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: Wucai and her middle brother Xiao, having lost their mother at a young age, attached themselves to the queen. The queen cultivated their loyalty through calculated kindness, and together they slandered the Crown Prince. On account of their slanders, the king repeatedly beat and flogged the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 元朔四年中，人有賊傷王后假母者，王疑太子使人傷之，笞太子。

translations.literal: In the middle of the fourth year of the Yuanshuo era, someone attacked and wounded the queen's foster mother. The king suspected the Crown Prince had sent someone to do it and had the Crown Prince flogged.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of the Yuanshuo era, someone attacked and wounded the queen's foster mother. The king suspected the Crown Prince was behind the attack and had him flogged.

sentences.zh: 後王病，太子時稱病不侍。

translations.literal: Later the king fell ill, and the Crown Prince at that time claimed to be sick and did not attend him.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when the king fell ill, the Crown Prince claimed to be sick himself and refused to attend upon him.

sentences.zh: 孝王后、無采惡太子：「太子實不病，自言病，有喜色。」

translations.literal: Xiao, the queen, and Wucai slandered the Crown Prince: 'The Crown Prince is not truly ill. He only claims to be ill, and there is a look of delight on his face.'
translations.idiomatic: Xiao, the queen, and Wucai slandered the Crown Prince, saying, 'The Crown Prince is not really ill. He only claims to be so, and there is a look of satisfaction on his face.'

sentences.zh: 王大怒，欲廢太子，立其弟孝。

translations.literal: The king was furious and wished to depose the Crown Prince and install his younger brother Xiao in his place.
translations.idiomatic: The king was furious and resolved to depose the Crown Prince and install his younger brother Xiao in his place.

sentences.zh: 王后知王決廢太子，又欲并廢孝。

translations.literal: The queen knew the king had decided to depose the Crown Prince but also wished to remove Xiao as well.
translations.idiomatic: The queen, learning that the king had decided to depose the Crown Prince, schemed to have Xiao removed as well.

sentences.zh: 王后有侍者，善舞，王幸之，王后欲令侍者與孝亂以汙之，欲并廢兄弟而立其子廣代太子。

translations.literal: The queen had a maidservant who was a skilled dancer and whom the king favored. The queen wished to have this maidservant seduce Xiao to disgrace him, so that both brothers could be deposed and her own son Guang could be made Crown Prince in their stead.
translations.idiomatic: The queen had a maidservant who was a skilled dancer and whom the king favored. She plotted to have this maidservant seduce Xiao and compromise him, so that both brothers would be disgraced and her own son, Guang, could be made Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 太子爽知之，念后數惡己無已時，欲與亂以止其口。

translations.literal: Crown Prince Shuang learned of this and reflected that the queen's slanders against him would never cease. He wished to have an illicit affair with her to silence her.
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Shuang discovered the plot and reflected that the queen's slanders against him would never end. He decided to attempt to seduce her in order to silence her.

sentences.zh: 王后飲，太子前為壽，因據王后股，求與王后臥。

translations.literal: When the queen was drinking, the Crown Prince came forward to offer a toast. He then seized the queen's thigh and demanded to sleep with her.
translations.idiomatic: When the queen was drinking, the Crown Prince approached to offer a toast. He seized the queen by the thigh and demanded to lie with her.

sentences.zh: 王后怒，以告王。

translations.literal: The queen was furious and reported this to the king.
translations.idiomatic: The queen was enraged and reported the incident to the king.

sentences.zh: 王乃召，欲縛而笞之。

translations.literal: The king summoned the Crown Prince, intending to bind and flog him.
translations.idiomatic: The king summoned the Crown Prince, intending to have him bound and flogged.

sentences.zh: 太子知王常欲廢己立其弟孝，乃謂王曰：「孝與王御者姦，無采與奴姦，王彊食，請上書。」

translations.literal: The Crown Prince, knowing that the king had long wished to depose him and install his brother Xiao, said to the king: 'Xiao has been having an affair with Your Majesty's carriage attendant, and Wucai has been having an affair with a slave. I beg Your Majesty to eat well and maintain your health while I submit a memorial to the emperor.'
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince, knowing full well that the king had long wished to depose him in favor of Xiao, said to the king, 'Xiao has been carrying on an affair with one of Your Majesty's personal attendants, and Wucai has been carrying on with a slave. I beg Your Majesty to look after your health while I submit a memorial to the emperor.'

sentences.zh: 即倍王去。

translations.literal: He then turned his back on the king and left.
translations.idiomatic: With that, he turned his back on the king and walked out.

sentences.zh: 王使人止之，莫能禁，乃自駕追捕太子。

translations.literal: The king sent men to stop him, but none could restrain him. The king then drove out himself to pursue and capture the Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: The king sent men to stop him, but none could restrain him. The king then personally drove out to pursue and capture the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 太子妄惡言，王械系太子宮中。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince spoke reckless and vile words. The king had him placed in fetters and confined in the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince hurled wild and abusive words at his father. The king had him clapped in irons and confined within the palace.

sentences.zh: 孝日益親幸。

translations.literal: Xiao grew closer to the king's favor day by day.
translations.idiomatic: Day by day, Xiao rose higher in the king's favor.

sentences.zh: 王奇孝材能，乃佩之王印，號曰將軍，令居外宅，多給金錢，招致賓客。

translations.literal: The king marveled at Xiao's talent and bestowed upon him the royal seal, giving him the title of General. He ordered him to reside in a separate estate, provided him with ample gold and money, and allowed him to recruit retainers.
translations.idiomatic: Impressed by Xiao's ability, the king bestowed the royal seal upon him and granted him the title of General. He was given a separate residence, supplied with ample gold and money, and permitted to recruit retainers.

sentences.zh: 賓客來者，微知淮南、衡山有逆計，日夜從容勸之。

translations.literal: The retainers who came had some inkling that Huainan and Hengshan harbored treasonous plans, and day and night they subtly encouraged him.
translations.idiomatic: The retainers who gathered around him had some inkling of the treasonous plans afoot in Huainan and Hengshan, and day and night they subtly encouraged his ambitions.

sentences.zh: 王乃使孝客江都人救赫、陳喜作輣車鏃矢，刻天子璽，將相軍吏印。

translations.literal: The king then had Xiao's retainers, Jiu He and Chen Xi of Jiangdu, build assault wagons and arrowheads, and carve the seal of the Son of Heaven along with the seals of generals, ministers, and military officials.
translations.idiomatic: The king then ordered Xiao's retainers—Jiu He and Chen Xi of Jiangdu—to construct assault wagons and arrowheads, and to carve counterfeits of the imperial seal and the seals of generals, ministers, and military officials.

sentences.zh: 王日夜求壯士如周丘等，數稱引吳楚反時計畫，以約束。

translations.literal: Day and night, the king sought brave men like Zhou Qiu and others, frequently citing and discussing the stratagems used during the Wu-Chu rebellion, in order to coordinate their plans.
translations.idiomatic: Day and night the king sought out daring men like Zhou Qiu, frequently invoking the stratagems of the Wu and Chu rebellion as models for their own plans.

sentences.zh: 衡山王非敢效淮南王求即天子位，畏淮南起并其國，以為淮南已西，發兵定江淮之閒而有之，望如是。

translations.literal: The King of Hengshan did not dare emulate the King of Huainan in seeking the position of Son of Heaven. He feared that when Huainan rose, it would annex his kingdom. His plan was that once Huainan had marched west, he would raise troops and seize control of the region between the Yangtze and the Huai for himself. Such were his aspirations.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Hengshan did not aspire, as the King of Huainan did, to seize the throne itself. He feared that when Huainan rose in revolt, it would swallow his kingdom. His plan was that once Huainan had marched west, he would raise his own troops and seize the lands between the Yangtze and the Huai. Such was the extent of his ambition.

sentences.zh: 元朔五年秋，衡山王當朝，六年過淮南，淮南王乃昆弟語，除前卻，約束反具。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the fifth year of the Yuanshuo era, the King of Hengshan was due at court. In the sixth year he passed through Huainan, and the King of Huainan spoke with him as a brother, putting aside their former estrangement, and they agreed upon the preparations for rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the fifth year of the Yuanshuo era, the King of Hengshan was due to attend court. In the sixth year he passed through Huainan, where the King of Huainan received him as a brother, setting aside their former estrangement. Together they agreed upon the final preparations for rebellion.

sentences.zh: 衡山王即上書謝病，上賜書不朝。

translations.literal: The King of Hengshan then submitted a memorial pleading illness, and the Emperor sent a letter excusing him from attending court.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Hengshan then submitted a memorial pleading illness, and the emperor granted him a letter excusing him from attending court.

sentences.zh: 元朔六年中，衡山王使人上書請廢太子爽，立孝為太子。

translations.literal: In the middle of the sixth year of the Yuanshuo era, the King of Hengshan sent someone to submit a memorial requesting the deposition of Crown Prince Shuang and the installation of Xiao as Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of the Yuanshuo era, the King of Hengshan sent a memorial requesting the removal of Crown Prince Shuang and the appointment of Xiao as Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 爽聞，即使所善白嬴之長安上書，言孝作輣車鏃矢，與王御者姦，欲以敗孝。

translations.literal: When Shuang heard of this, he sent his trusted associate Bai Ying to Chang'an to submit a memorial stating that Xiao had been building assault wagons and arrowheads and having an affair with the king's attendant, intending to ruin Xiao.
translations.idiomatic: When Shuang learned of this, he sent his trusted associate Bai Ying to Chang'an to submit a memorial accusing Xiao of building assault wagons and arrowheads and of having an affair with one of the king's attendants, hoping to bring Xiao down.

sentences.zh: 白嬴至長安，未及上書，吏捕嬴，以淮南事系。

translations.literal: Bai Ying arrived in Chang'an but before he could submit the memorial, he was arrested by the authorities and imprisoned in connection with the Huainan case.
translations.idiomatic: Bai Ying reached Chang'an, but before he could submit the memorial, he was arrested by the authorities and imprisoned in connection with the Huainan affair.

sentences.zh: 王聞爽使白嬴上書，恐言國陰事，即上書反告太子爽所為不道棄市罪事。

translations.literal: When the king heard that Shuang had sent Bai Ying to submit a memorial, he feared the kingdom's secret plans would be revealed. He immediately submitted his own memorial, counter-accusing Crown Prince Shuang of crimes deserving public execution.
translations.idiomatic: When the king learned that Shuang had sent Bai Ying to submit a memorial, he feared the kingdom's secret plans would be exposed. He immediately submitted his own memorial, counter-accusing Crown Prince Shuang of capital crimes.

sentences.zh: 事下沛郡治。

translations.literal: The case was referred to the Governor of Pei Commandery for investigation.
translations.idiomatic: The case was referred to the Governor of Pei Commandery for investigation.

sentences.zh: 元（朔七）［狩元］年冬，有司公卿下沛郡求捕所與淮南謀反者未得，得陳喜於衡山王子孝家。

translations.literal: In the winter of the first year of the Yuanshou era, the authorities and ministers searched the Pei Commandery for accomplices in the Huainan conspiracy. They had not found them, but then discovered Chen Xi at the home of the King of Hengshan's son Xiao.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the first year of the Yuanshou era, authorities searching Pei Commandery for accomplices in the Huainan conspiracy had come up empty-handed, when they discovered Chen Xi at the residence of the King of Hengshan's son Xiao.

sentences.zh: 吏劾孝首匿喜。

translations.literal: The officials charged Xiao with harboring Chen Xi as the principal offender.
translations.idiomatic: The officials charged Xiao with knowingly harboring the fugitive Chen Xi.

sentences.zh: 孝以為陳喜雅數與王計謀反，恐其發之，聞律先自告除其罪，又疑太子使白嬴上書發其事，即先自告，告所與謀反者救赫、陳喜等。

translations.literal: Xiao realized that Chen Xi had always been deeply involved in the king's plots for rebellion and feared these would be exposed. He had heard that the law absolved those who confessed first, and he also suspected the Crown Prince had sent Bai Ying to submit a memorial exposing the plot. So Xiao confessed first, denouncing those who had conspired in the rebellion: Jiu He, Chen Xi, and others.
translations.idiomatic: Xiao knew that Chen Xi had been deeply involved in the king's rebellion plots and feared their exposure. He recalled that the law granted immunity to those who confessed first, and he suspected that the Crown Prince had sent Bai Ying to expose the plot. So Xiao raced to confess first, naming Jiu He, Chen Xi, and the other conspirators.

sentences.zh: 廷尉治驗，公卿請逮捕衡山王治之。

translations.literal: The Commandant of Justice investigated and verified the charges. The ministers requested permission to arrest and try the King of Hengshan.
translations.idiomatic: The Commandant of Justice investigated and verified the charges. The ministers petitioned for the arrest and trial of the King of Hengshan.

sentences.zh: 天子曰：「勿捕。」

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven said: 'Do not arrest him.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, 'Do not arrest him.'

sentences.zh: 遣中尉安、大行息即問王，王具以情實對。

translations.literal: He dispatched Colonel An and Grand Herald Xi to question the king at once. The king gave a complete and truthful account.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched Colonel An and Grand Herald Xi to question the king in person. The king gave a full and truthful confession.

sentences.zh: 吏皆圍王宮而守之。

translations.literal: The officials then surrounded and guarded the royal palace.
translations.idiomatic: The officials surrounded the royal palace and placed it under guard.

sentences.zh: 中尉大行還，以聞，公卿請遣宗正、大行與沛郡雜治王。

translations.literal: The Colonel and the Grand Herald returned and reported to the throne. The ministers requested that the Director of the Imperial Clan and the Grand Herald be dispatched together with the Governor of Pei Commandery to jointly try the king.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel and the Grand Herald returned and reported their findings. The ministers petitioned that the Director of the Imperial Clan and the Grand Herald be sent to jointly try the king alongside the Governor of Pei Commandery.

sentences.zh: 王聞，即自剄殺。

translations.literal: When the king heard of this, he cut his own throat and died.
translations.idiomatic: When the king learned of this, he cut his own throat and died.

sentences.zh: 孝先自告反，除其罪；

translations.literal: Xiao had confessed to the rebellion first and was cleared of that crime;
translations.idiomatic: Because Xiao had been the first to confess to the rebellion, he was pardoned for that offense;

sentences.zh: 坐與王御婢姦，棄市。

translations.literal: but he was convicted of having an affair with the king's maidservant and was publicly executed.
translations.idiomatic: however, he was convicted of having an affair with one of the king's maidservants and was publicly executed.

sentences.zh: 王后徐來亦坐蠱殺前王后乘舒，及太子爽坐王告不孝，皆棄市。

translations.literal: Queen Xu Lai was also convicted of using sorcery to kill the former Queen Cheng Shu, and Crown Prince Shuang was convicted based on the king's accusation of unfiliality. Both were publicly executed.
translations.idiomatic: Queen Xu Lai was convicted of having used sorcery to murder the former Queen Cheng Shu, and Crown Prince Shuang was convicted on the basis of the king's charge of unfilial conduct. Both were publicly executed.

sentences.zh: 諸與衡山王謀反者皆族。

translations.literal: All those who had conspired with the King of Hengshan in rebellion were executed along with their clans.
translations.idiomatic: All those who had conspired with the King of Hengshan were executed along with their entire clans.

sentences.zh: 國除為衡山郡。

translations.literal: The kingdom was abolished and made into the Commandery of Hengshan.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom was abolished and converted into the Commandery of Hengshan.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：《詩》之所謂「戎狄是膺，荊舒是懲」，信哉是言也。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian remarks: The Book of Odes says, 'The Rong and Di he smote, the Jing and Shu he punished.' These words are truly to be believed.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: The Book of Odes declares, 'The Rong and the Di he struck down, the Jing and the Shu he chastised.' How true these words ring.

sentences.zh: 淮南、衡山親為骨肉，疆土千里，列為諸侯，不務遵蕃臣職以承輔天子，而專挾邪僻之計，謀為畔逆，仍父子再亡國，各不終其身，為天下笑。

translations.literal: The houses of Huainan and Hengshan were the flesh and bone of the imperial family. With territories stretching a thousand li and ranked among the vassal lords, they did not devote themselves to the duties of a loyal vassal in support of the Son of Heaven, but instead harbored wicked and treacherous schemes, plotting rebellion. Father and son alike, they twice lost their kingdoms, and none of them lived out their natural lives, becoming laughingstocks for the entire realm.
translations.idiomatic: The houses of Huainan and Hengshan were the emperor's own flesh and blood, with territories stretching a thousand li and the rank of vassal lords. Yet they did not devote themselves to the duties of loyal vassals in service to the Son of Heaven, but instead harbored only wicked and treacherous designs, plotting rebellion. Across two generations, father and son, they twice brought ruin upon their kingdoms. None of them died a natural death, and they became the laughingstock of the entire empire.

sentences.zh: 此非獨王過也，亦其俗薄，臣下漸靡使然也。

translations.literal: This was not solely the kings' fault. The debased customs and the gradual corruption of their ministers and subjects also contributed to this outcome.
translations.idiomatic: This was not the fault of the kings alone. The coarseness of local customs and the gradual corruption of their ministers and subjects were also to blame.

sentences.zh: 夫荊楚彊勇輕悍，好作亂，乃自古記之矣。

translations.literal: The people of Jing and Chu are strong, brave, impetuous, and fierce, and are fond of making trouble. This has been recorded since ancient times.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Jing and Chu have always been strong, bold, impetuous, and fierce, with a propensity for disorder. This has been noted in the records since antiquity.
